title: SMACH YAML to Mermaid transpiler with container outcome handling
purpose: Converts SMACH state machine YAML definitions into Mermaid flowchart syntax, properly handling nested containers and their outcome nodes with chaining.
module_context: Exports transpile function to convert YAML to Mermaid, getStateProperties to retrieve state metadata, and isSmachYaml to detect SMACH format. Uses js-yaml for parsing and maintains stateProperties for click-to-view popups.

steps.content: Sanitize the input name by replacing non-alphanumeric characters with underscores.
If a prefix is provided, concatenate it with the sanitized name using an underscore.
Return the resulting node ID string.

methods.name: nodeDeclaration
methods.normalized_params: (id: string, label: string, type: string): string

steps.content: Determine the shape for the node based on the state type.
Generate the appropriate Mermaid node syntax for the determined shape.
Return the formatted node declaration string.

methods.name: processStates
methods.normalized_params: (states: object, lines: string[], externalLinks: string[], prefix: string, initialState: string, indent: string, parentOutcomes: string[], parentOutcomePrefix: string): void

steps.content: Iterate through each state in the input states object.
Generate a unique node ID for the current state using the prefix.
Determine the shape for the current state type.
Store properties for the current state in the global properties store.
IF the state is a container type, render it as a subgraph with internal structure.
ELSE render the state as a leaf node with appropriate shape.
Process transitions for the current state and add appropriate links to the output.
Parse the input YAML text using js-yaml library.
Validate that the parsed YAML contains a valid smach_diagram structure.
Extract the root StateMachine from the diagram.
Initialize output lines and external links arrays for Mermaid generation.
Add a start node and link it to the root machine's initial state.
Process the root machine's states recursively using the processStates function.
Add outcome nodes for the root machine.
Append external links to the output lines.
Return the complete Mermaid code and state properties.
Look up and return the properties stored for the given node ID.
IF no properties exist for the node, return null.
Return a copy of all stored state properties.
Check if the input text contains the smach_diagram pattern using regex matching.
Return true if the pattern is found, otherwise return false.
